commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The forecast items lack hover states, which would improve the user experience for interactive weather widgets.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover background color or scale effect to the forecast day columns.

issues.description: Weather icons are currently rendered as text emojis, which may lack consistent screen reader announcement or visual scaling.
issues.fix: Wrap icons in an aria-hidden span and add an aria-label to the parent element describing the weather condition.

issues.description: The current layout for humidity and wind speed uses 'justify: around', which can look unaligned on very wide cards.
issues.fix: Use a grid or explicit flex-basis to ensure consistent spacing between metrics.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and correctly maps all required props. It provides a solid baseline, though adding subtle micro-interactions to the forecast strip would elevate it to production-ready status.

evaluation.critique: The component has good hierarchy and data presentation, but uses hardcoded fallback hex colors in inline styles instead of relying purely on design tokens, and lacks interactive polish (no hover states, transitions, or responsive behavior for the forecast strip). The forecast items also need better spacing tokens for gap values.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Grid cells have no hover state, making them feel static despite being interactive.
issues.fix: Add a hover effect (e.g., scale or border color change) to the grid items.

issues.description: The search input is a raw Box component without proper styles or ARIA associations, and grid cells are not focusable or keyboard-navigable.
issues.fix: Use the library's Input primitive for the search field and add tabIndex={0} and keyboard event handlers (onKeyDown) to the grid cells.

issues.description: The search input lacks visual feedback and proper styling.
issues.fix: Apply consistent padding, border, and focus states to the search input.

issues.description: Missing a clear legend description; the badges act as placeholders but don't visually represent the mapping clearly.
issues.fix: Add a heading above the category list and ensure colors in the legend match the cells perfectly.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements the complex grid layout requirement using the specified data points. It is functional and well-structured, but it suffers from a lack of focus on accessibility and polished interaction states that would make it feel like a professional tool.

evaluation.critique: The component uses CSS Grid correctly for the periodic table layout and renders data properly from props, but has several issues: the search input lacks proper styling (raw HTML input instead of ggui component), hardcoded pixel values (32, 24, 8) for spacing instead of design tokens, and missing interactive polish (no focus states, button styling). The design tokens are partially used but inconsistently—gaps use raw pixels and the input element bypasses ggui's form primitives.
